explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `lie in` 不是字面上的「躺在裡面」，而是指「關鍵在於／取決於」。整句意思是：希望在於無產階級。
generatedExample: The solution to the problem lies in better communication.

explanationZhTw: `proles` 是 `proletarians` 的簡稱，指「無產階級、勞工階層」，是《一九八四》裡的社會階級用語。
generatedExample: The proles in the city struggled with low wages and long hours.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「下層平民／工人階級」，是《1984》裡對某一群人的專稱，不是一般字面上的詞義。
generatedExample: The proles were the only group large enough to challenge the regime.

explanationZhTw: 形容人群像蜂群一樣大量聚集、四處湧動；這裡帶有貶義，強調被統治者數量龐大但被忽視。
generatedExample: The platform was filled with swarming commuters before the train arrived.

explanationZhTw: 推翻、顛覆，通常用於政權、政府或權威被迫下台。
generatedExample: The dictator was overthrown after weeks of protests.

explanationZhTw: 集合、聚集在一起；這裡指秘密成員無法大規模會合。
generatedExample: The committee will assemble at nine o'clock tomorrow morning.

explanationZhTw: 聲調的起伏或變化；這裡不是「彎曲」的字面義，而是指說話語氣裡透露出的意思。
generatedExample: I could hear suspicion in the inflexion of his voice.

explanationZhTw: 意識到自己的力量；這裡指如果工人階級覺醒，就會知道自己有能力改變局勢。
generatedExample: Once the workers became conscious of their own strength, they demanded better pay.

explanationZhTw: 起義、反抗；常用來說人民或群體起來對抗壓迫者。
generatedExample: The villagers rose up against the corrupt mayor.

explanationZhTw: 比喻輕易甩掉麻煩或束縛，畫面感很強；重點是「很容易就能擺脫」。
generatedExample: She shook off the criticism like a horse shaking off flies and kept going.

explanationZhTw: 徹底摧毀；這裡不是字面上的炸碎，而是說把政權完全打垮。
generatedExample: One mistake could blow the whole plan to pieces.

explanationZhTw: 突然想到、想到某個主意進入腦中。
generatedExample: It never occurred to me that he would refuse the offer.

explanationZhTw: crowded 表示「擁擠的、塞滿人的」；這裡是在描寫街道上人很多，空間很擠。
generatedExample: The train was crowded during rush hour.

explanationZhTw: tremendous 表示「非常大的、極強烈的」；這裡形容喊聲的規模和力量很驚人。
generatedExample: We heard a tremendous noise outside.

explanationZhTw: burst here 是「突然爆發、猛然傳出」；不是單純的「破裂」，而是聲音突然衝出來。
generatedExample: Laughter burst from the back of the room.

explanationZhTw: formidable 在這裡不是「可敬的」，而是「令人畏懼的、強而有壓迫感的」；用來加強那種聲音的震撼力。
generatedExample: The storm was a formidable threat to the boats.

explanationZhTw: break loose 表示「掙脫束縛、失去控制而爆發」；這裡是在說群眾開始失控。
generatedExample: The crowd suddenly broke loose and rushed forward.

explanationZhTw: jostled 表示「被推撞、被擠來擠去」；常用來描述人群中的粗魯碰撞。
generatedExample: I got jostled on the subway platform.

explanationZhTw: make off 表示「匆忙離開、帶著東西逃走」；這裡有趁亂把平底鍋帶走的意思。
generatedExample: The thief made off with her bag.

explanationZhTw: clamoured 表示「大聲吵鬧、喧嚷著要求」；帶有持續、嘈雜的抗議感。
generatedExample: Fans clamoured for tickets after the announcement.

explanationZhTw: in reserve 這裡是「留作備用、藏著未拿出來」；不是一般的「預備部隊」字面意思。
generatedExample: She kept a few spare batteries in reserve.

explanationZhTw: outburst 表示「突然的一陣爆發」；可以是情緒、聲音或行動的突然爆出。
generatedExample: He had an outburst of anger in the meeting.

explanationZhTw: get hold of / got hold of 表示「抓住、搶到、取得」；這裡是兩個人都抓住同一個鍋子。
generatedExample: I finally got hold of her after lunch.

explanationZhTw: come off 在這裡表示「脫落、掉下來」；不是「成功與否」的常見意思。
generatedExample: One wheel came off during the trip.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「become conscious」不是單純指「醒著」，而是指「覺醒、意識到自己的處境」；在這段話中帶有政治或思想上的覺醒意味。
generatedExample: People often need time and education before they become conscious of unfair treatment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「rebelled」指「反抗、起義」，也就是對權威、制度或控制發動抵抗。
generatedExample: The students rebelled against the school rule they thought was unjust.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「become conscious」不是單純指「醒著」，而是指「覺醒、意識到自己的處境」；在這段話中帶有政治或思想上的覺醒意味。
generatedExample: People often need time and education before they become conscious of unfair treatment.

explanationZhTw: 表示「事實上」或「說來也是」，用來補充一個看似插話的真實情況。
generatedExample: I don't need a new laptop, as a matter of fact.

explanationZhTw: 《雙重思想》：同時接受兩套互相矛盾、但都被當成真的說法或信念。
generatedExample: The manager's doublethink let him praise teamwork while rewarding rivalry.

explanationZhTw: 使其處於受控制、被壓制的狀態。
generatedExample: The regime kept the population in subjection through fear.

explanationZhTw: 沒有人管時，任其自行發展或照原樣行事。
generatedExample: Left to themselves, the kids turned the living room into a fort.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「記下來作為監控或處理對象」；不是一般的「降低價格」。
generatedExample: The investigators were marking down everyone who entered the building.

explanationZhTw: 可被拿來訴諸或煽動，用來影響他們。
generatedExample: That fear could be appealed to whenever the campaign needed support.

explanationZhTw: 指一個自成系統、藏在大環境中的小社會或地下圈子。
generatedExample: The port had a world-within-a-world of smugglers and fixers.

explanationZhTw: 性關係較隨便、不專一；在此指這種行為不會被懲罰。
generatedExample: The film explores the consequences of promiscuity.

explanationZhTw: 低到不會被懷疑，也就是不被當成威脅。
generatedExample: Because he looked harmless, he was beneath suspicion.

explanationZhTw: `varicose ulcer` 是醫學用語，指和靜脈曲張有關的潰瘍。這屬於比較專門的身體健康詞彙。
generatedExample: The nurse cleaned the varicose ulcer and changed the dressing.

explanationZhTw: `invariably` 表示「總是、每次都如此；沒有例外」。這裡是在說，思緒最後一定會回到同一個問題上。
generatedExample: She invariably arrives ten minutes early for meetings.

explanationZhTw: `come back to` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「再回到某個話題、想法或重點」。
generatedExample: We kept coming back to the same question during the discussion.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `it ran` 不是「它跑了」，而是指「說法是這樣／據說」，用來引出敘述來源。
generatedExample: As the old story ran, a hidden tunnel led under the river.

explanationZhTw: `Glorious Revolution` 是英國歷史上的「光榮革命」，屬於專有歷史名詞，不是字面上的「光榮的革命」。
generatedExample: We studied the Glorious Revolution in history class.

explanationZhTw: `miserable` 表示「悲慘的、很不舒服的、令人難受的」。
generatedExample: I felt miserable after hearing the bad news.

explanationZhTw: `flogged` 意思是「用鞭子抽打」。
generatedExample: In the old prison story, the guards flogged anyone who disobeyed.

explanationZhTw: `poverty` 是「貧困、窮困」，指缺乏金錢和基本生活資源。
generatedExample: The charity works to reduce poverty in remote villages.

explanationZhTw: `look after` 是片語動詞，意思是「照顧、照料、伺候」。
generatedExample: Can you look after my cat while I'm away?

explanationZhTw: `capitalists` 指「資本家」，也就是擁有資本、土地、工廠或大量財富的人。
generatedExample: Some capitalists invest in new businesses to grow their wealth.

explanationZhTw: `uniform` 是「制服」，指某個群體固定穿的服裝。
generatedExample: The students wore a blue uniform every weekday.

explanationZhTw: `slave` 是「奴隸」；在這裡也強調「完全受制於人、沒有自由」。
generatedExample: He said he refused to become a slave to debt.

explanationZhTw: `disobeyed` 是「違抗、不服從」，通常指不照命令或規定做。
generatedExample: The soldier was punished because he disobeyed orders.

explanationZhTw: `cringe and bow` 表示「卑躬屈膝、低聲下氣地鞠躬」，帶有非常恭順甚至屈辱的語氣。
generatedExample: He had to cringe and bow whenever the boss entered the room.

explanationZhTw: `take off` 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「脫下」。
generatedExample: Please take off your shoes before you enter.

explanationZhTw: `address him as` 這裡不是「住址」，而是「稱呼他為、用……來稱呼他」。
generatedExample: The teacher asked us to address him as Mr. Chen.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「目錄、清單」的意思，指一連串已知的項目。
generatedExample: She checked the catalogue before ordering the book.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `lawn` 不是草地，而是細薄亞麻布；`lawn sleeves` 指主教禮服的袖子。
generatedExample: The bishop appeared in lawn sleeves during the ceremony.

explanationZhTw: `ermine` 是白貂毛；`ermine robes` 在這裡指鑲有白貂毛邊的法官或高階禮服長袍。
generatedExample: The judge wore ermine robes in court.

explanationZhTw: 古代公開示眾的刑具，會把人固定住並當眾羞辱。
generatedExample: The thief was punished in the pillory.

explanationZhTw: 古代刑具，把人的腳固定住，使人無法走動。
generatedExample: He was left in the stocks overnight.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是現代健身器材，而是過去用來勞役或懲罰人的裝置。
generatedExample: The prisoners were forced to work on a treadmill.

explanationZhTw: 九尾鞭，一種由多條皮條組成的鞭刑工具。
generatedExample: The captain threatened him with a cat-o'-nine-tails.

explanationZhTw: 拉丁語術語，意思是「初夜權」，指封建領主被傳說賦予的性特權。
generatedExample: The essay discussed the legend of jus primae noctis.

explanationZhTw: 教科書。
generatedExample: This textbook is written for young readers.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「資本家」的意思，指擁有工廠和資本的人。
generatedExample: The capitalist owned several factories.

explanationZhTw: 表示「處境比較好、過得比較好」；這裡是在比較革命前後的生活狀況。
generatedExample: She was better off after she changed jobs.

explanationZhTw: 表示「相反的證據或說法」；用來指出前面的看法可能不成立。
generatedExample: There was no proof to the contrary.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「無聲的抗議」；這裡指一種沒有說出口、只在內心或身體感受到的不滿。
generatedExample: Her silence was a mute protest against the decision.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無法忍受的、糟到受不了的」。
generatedExample: The heat inside the bus was intolerable.

explanationZhTw: 指「空曠、單調、缺乏生氣的狀態」；這裡不是單純指「裸露」，而是抽象的貧乏感。
generatedExample: The bareness of the room made it feel cold.

explanationZhTw: 表示「陰暗骯髒、灰撲撲的樣子」。
generatedExample: The dinginess of the hallway needed a deep clean.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無精打采、提不起勁」的狀態。
generatedExample: Her listlessness disappeared after a good night's sleep.

explanationZhTw: 表示「和……毫不相像、完全不像」。
generatedExample: The final design bore no resemblance to the first sketch.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「源源不絕地流出、播送出來」；語氣強調大量持續地出現。
generatedExample: Warm air streamed out of the open door.

explanationZhTw: 表示「費力地硬撐著做、艱難地熬過」；常帶有吃力、沉重的感覺。
generatedExample: I spent the morning slogging through paperwork.

explanationZhTw: 表示「厚著臉皮討要、蹭來」；這裡是帶點貧困或拮据感的用法。
generatedExample: He kept cadging money from his friends.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「建立、塑造、設立」；指政黨所打造的理想樣貌。
generatedExample: They set up a new system for approvals.

explanationZhTw: 表示「永遠地、持續不斷地」；在這裡強調永無止境的工作與鬥爭。
generatedExample: He was perpetually late for class.

explanationZhTw: 表示「拖著腳步來回走動」；通常帶有疲憊、無力的感覺。
generatedExample: People shuffled to and fro in the station corridor.

explanationZhTw: 表示「勉強修補過的、打過補丁的」；這裡描寫破舊房子。
generatedExample: He wore patched-up boots in winter.

explanationZhTw: 表示「和……混在一起、摻雜在一起」；這裡是視覺意象彼此交疊。
generatedExample: The facts were mixed up with rumors.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「拉小提琴」，而是指「笨拙地擺弄、瞎弄」某樣東西。
generatedExample: He was fiddling with the radio until it worked again.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上「打瘀」耳朵，而是形容聲音或宣傳非常刺耳、反覆轟炸，讓人聽得難受。
generatedExample: The loud ads bruised my ears after an hour.

explanationZhTw: 「statistics」指統計數據或統計資料。這裡是指黨用來宣傳的數字。
generatedExample: The report shows statistics about air quality.

explanationZhTw: 「prove or disprove」指證明或推翻某個說法。
generatedExample: We need evidence to prove or disprove the claim.

explanationZhTw: 「proles」是本書中的專門用語，指底層平民或無產階級。
generatedExample: The proles lived in crowded districts outside the center.

explanationZhTw: 「literate」指有讀寫能力的、識字的。
generatedExample: Only a few people in the village were literate at that time.

explanationZhTw: 「infant mortality rate」指嬰兒死亡率，也就是嬰兒死亡的比例。
generatedExample: The infant mortality rate dropped after the clinic opened.

explanationZhTw: 「went on」在這裡表示「繼續下去、持續不斷地說或列舉」。
generatedExample: He complained about the weather and went on for ten minutes.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，意思是資訊不足、變數太多，因此很難得出答案。
generatedExample: Without the budget numbers, the plan is a single equation with two unknowns.

explanationZhTw: 「accepted without question」指不加懷疑就接受，沒有追問真假。
generatedExample: The rumor was accepted without question by many readers.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「pure fantasy」表示完全是幻想，毫無真實根據。
generatedExample: His story about winning the lottery turned out to be pure fantasy.

explanationZhTw: 「For all he knew」意思是「就他所知；從他掌握的資訊來看」。
generatedExample: For all she knew, the train had already left.

explanationZhTw: 比喻「變得模糊、消失不見」；這裡不是指真正的霧，而是指過去像被抹掉一樣難以辨認。
generatedExample: After the long argument, their plans faded into mist.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「被抹去、被消除」的意思，指記憶、歷史或痕跡被有意刪除，而不只是一般的擦掉。
generatedExample: The old records were erased from the database.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，表示「抹除、刪除」的動作或結果；在這裡特別指過去被消除得幾乎沒有痕跡。
generatedExample: The erasure of the files caused a serious problem.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「偽造、捏造、竄改」；在這裡是指把事實或證據故意改成假的行為。
generatedExample: The audit uncovered a falsification of the sales figures.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「不管怎樣／無論如何」，用來修正、退一步或補充前面的說法。
generatedExample: I think he left early, or at any rate before noon.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「分開、分手」的意思，通常指兩個人結束關係或各自離開。
generatedExample: They parted after college and lost touch for years.

explanationZhTw: 「相關的、切題的」；這裡指真正有關鍵作用的日期，而不是表面上看起來的日期。
generatedExample: Please give me the relevant information for this case.

explanationZhTw: 「purges」指政治上的大清洗，通常是以逮捕、開除或處決等方式，系統性地清除被視為威脅的人。在這裡是極權政權內部整肅的意思。
generatedExample: The new regime launched purges against anyone suspected of disloyalty.

explanationZhTw: 「wiped out」在這裡不是單純「擦掉」，而是「徹底消滅、全部清除」；「once and for all」加強語氣，表示永遠地、徹底地。
generatedExample: The old militia was wiped out once and for all after the final battle.

explanationZhTw: 「be exposed as + 名詞」表示「被揭發為／被證實是」。這裡是說這些人被公開揭露成叛徒。
generatedExample: The manager was exposed as a fraud after months of investigation.

explanationZhTw: 「brought forth」在此不是一般的「帶出來」，而是帶上公開場面、被押出來接受審問或審判的意思，帶有強迫意味。
generatedExample: The witness was brought forth to face the tribunal.

explanationZhTw: 「incriminate」是「使...牽連入罪」；「incriminate themselves」表示他們被迫自證其罪，或在供詞中把自己說成有罪。
generatedExample: Under pressure, he started to incriminate himself during the interview.

explanationZhTw: 「embezzlement」是「挪用公款、侵吞他人或公家財物」的正式說法，法律語氣很強。
generatedExample: She was arrested for embezzlement of company money.

explanationZhTw: 「public funds」指公共資金、政府或人民共同持有的錢。這裡搭配「embezzlement」表示侵吞公款。
generatedExample: The audit found misuse of public funds.

explanationZhTw: 「intrigues」在這裡不是一般的「有趣事情」，而是「陰謀、勾結、暗中策畫的政治計謀」。
generatedExample: The report described secret intrigues inside the court.

explanationZhTw: 「pardoned」是「被赦免、被原諒」；在法律或政治脈絡下，表示免除處罰。
generatedExample: The governor pardoned the prisoner on humanitarian grounds.

explanationZhTw: 「reinstated」是「恢復職位、恢復原狀」；這裡是被重新納入黨內。
generatedExample: She was reinstated after the investigation cleared her name.

explanationZhTw: 「sinecure」指沒有多少實際工作、卻待遇不錯的閒職或掛名職位。文中強調這些職位只是外表重要。
generatedExample: He accepted a sinecure that required little real work.

explanationZhTw: 「abject」在這裡表示「卑微屈辱的、極其可憐的」，帶有自我貶抑和失格的語氣。
generatedExample: He made an abject apology after the public scandal.

explanationZhTw: 「analysing」在這裡是「分析、剖析」；它不是單純描述，而是試圖找出行為背後的原因和邏輯。
generatedExample: The essay is analysing the causes of the conflict.

explanationZhTw: 「defection」指「背離、叛離」，通常是從某個政黨、陣營、國家或信念中轉向對立面。
generatedExample: His defection shocked the rest of the team.

explanationZhTw: 「make amends」是固定說法，意思是「彌補過失、補救錯誤」。通常指用行動修正先前造成的傷害。
generatedExample: He called his sister to make amends for what he had said.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是指「他們獲釋、被放出來」；release 作名詞，常用於從監禁、拘留或束縛中恢復自由。
generatedExample: After her release, she wrote to her family every week.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是「用餘光看見」，不是正眼盯著看。
generatedExample: I noticed the cat out of the corner of my eye while I was reading.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單指「古物」，而是比喻「舊時代殘存下來的人或事」，帶有歷史殘影的意思。
generatedExample: The old soldiers were relics of a vanished era.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「遺留下來、剩下來」。
generatedExample: We had some rice left over from dinner.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「迷人的光環、浪漫化的吸引力」，帶有被美化過的感覺。
generatedExample: The glamour of city life faded after a few months.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「地下」的字面意思，而是指「秘密進行的、地下化的」活動或運動。
generatedExample: They worked for an underground newspaper during the war.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「模糊的、不清楚的」，這裡是指記憶中的事實和日期開始變得混亂。
generatedExample: The photo came out blurry because I moved the camera.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「被政府或法律視為非法的人、逃亡者」；不只是普通的壞人。
generatedExample: The film tells the story of outlaws hiding in the mountains.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，指「碰不得、難以接近或對付的人」，帶有危險和禁忌感。
generatedExample: In that company, the founders were treated like untouchables.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是「落入……手中、被……抓住或控制」。
generatedExample: The secret file fell into the hands of the enemy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻說法，意思接近「送回墳墓、讓人死去」；語氣很強烈，也帶有宿命感。
generatedExample: That endless war sent too many young people back to the grave.

explanationZhTw: 表示「在……附近／接近……」；這裡是說連出現在這類人的附近都不明智。
generatedExample: We stayed in the neighbourhood of the station so we could leave early.

explanationZhTw: 指「招牌特色」或「特產」；這裡是咖啡館的招牌飲品。
generatedExample: The chef's speciality is a soup made with local mushrooms.

explanationZhTw: 指「諷刺漫畫家／漫畫諷刺畫家」，專門用誇張筆法畫人物或事件。
generatedExample: She worked as a caricaturist for a newspaper before becoming an illustrator.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「煽動／激起大眾輿論或情緒」，讓原本的看法變得更激烈。
generatedExample: The speech was designed to inflame popular opinion against the new law.

explanationZhTw: 指「翻炒舊內容、重複老調」，通常帶有缺乏新意的意思。
generatedExample: His article was just a rehashing of arguments people had heard before.

explanationZhTw: 是片語動詞，表示「緊抓著不放；堅持守住」。
generatedExample: He clung to his old routine even after moving to a new city.

explanationZhTw: 是慣用語，表示事情「就在眼前、當場」發生；這裡強調他看起來正逐漸在眼前瓦解。
generatedExample: The painting seemed to change before our eyes as the light shifted.

explanationZhTw: 形容聲音單薄、尖細、帶金屬感，常有刺耳、不自然的效果。
generatedExample: The radio had a tinny sound that made the song hard to enjoy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指液體，而是形容聲音像細流一樣慢慢傳出、若有似無地流動。
generatedExample: Soft laughter was trickling through the hallway.

explanationZhTw: 指「沒有人吩咐就自動做了」；這裡表示服務生主動送來新的酒杯。
generatedExample: Uncommanded, she opened the window when the room felt too hot.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「擺放整齊、排列好」。
generatedExample: He set out the tools before beginning the repair.

explanationZhTw: 這是非常不尋常的比喻，把聲音說成有「黃色」的感覺；可理解為尖銳、刺耳、帶病態或令人不舒服的音色。
generatedExample: She described the whistle as a white note, thin and painfully bright.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `sold` 不是一般的「賣出」，而是指「出賣、背叛」；整句是在說彼此互相背叛。
generatedExample: He sold his friends to protect himself.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `lie` 不是「說謊」，而是文學用法的「位於、躺在、存在於某處」。
generatedExample: The old books lie on the shelf by the window.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `lie` 不是「說謊」，而是文學用法的「位於、躺在、存在於某處」。
generatedExample: The old books lie on the shelf by the window.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `stirred` 不是「攪動」，而是表示「動一下、挪動」。整句意思是三個人完全沒有動。
generatedExample: The cat never stirred while the room was noisy.

explanationZhTw: `glanced` 表示「匆匆一瞥、快速看一眼」。`glanced again` 就是「又看了一眼」。
generatedExample: She glanced again at the clock and sighed.

explanationZhTw: `full of tears` 形容眼睛裡含著淚水，表示快要哭出來或已經在哭。
generatedExample: His eyes were full of tears when he read the letter.

explanationZhTw: `for the first time` 表示「第一次」。這裡強調他之前從未注意到這件事。
generatedExample: For the first time, I understood why she was upset.

explanationZhTw: `inward shudder` 不是外在真的發抖，而是指內心突然一陣顫動、發寒或不安的感覺。
generatedExample: He felt an inward shudder when he heard the news.

explanationZhTw: 又一次被逮捕；`re-` 表示「再次」。
generatedExample: The suspect was re-arrested after new evidence was found.

explanationZhTw: `posterity` 指「後代、未來世代」；整個片語表示「作為給後人的警惕案例」。
generatedExample: The ruined factory stands as a warning to posterity.

explanationZhTw: 笨重地掉出來；這裡形容文件從管道裡不穩地落到桌上。
generatedExample: A bundle of letters flopped out of the envelope when she opened it.

explanationZhTw: 氣壓傳送管；用壓縮空氣把紙張或小物件送到別處的管道。
generatedExample: The office sent the forms through the pneumatic tube.

explanationZhTw: 悄悄塞進去；表示有人把那張紙故意或偷偷放進其他文件之中。
generatedExample: Someone slipped a handwritten note in among the brochures.

explanationZhTw: 把它展平；指把摺皺的紙弄平。
generatedExample: She flattened it out before reading the torn page.

explanationZhTw: `confess` 是「承認、坦白」；這裡指在審訊中承認自己做過某事。
generatedExample: After a long silence, he confessed that he had broken the vase.

explanationZhTw: `stick in someone's memory` 是片語，表示「讓人記得很清楚、久久忘不掉」。
generatedExample: The smell of the sea has stuck in my memory ever since that trip.

explanationZhTw: `chance to be` 在這裡不是「有機會是」，而是「碰巧是、剛好是」；帶有偶然性。
generatedExample: She chanced to be at the station when the announcement was made.

explanationZhTw: `on record` 表示「有正式記錄的、留有檔案的」。
generatedExample: The decision is now on record and cannot be denied.

explanationZhTw: `countless` 是常見形容詞，表示「無數的、多到數不清的」。
generatedExample: We walked through countless narrow streets before finding the cafe.

explanationZhTw: 「discovery」這裡是「新發現、發現到的事實」；句中意思是這件事本身並不算新發現。
generatedExample: Her report was a useful discovery, but it did not surprise the team.

explanationZhTw: 「wiped out」在這裡不是擦掉，而是指「被徹底消滅、殺光、清除」。
generatedExample: The village was nearly wiped out by the flood.

explanationZhTw: 「purges」在政治語境中指「大清洗、肅清」，不是一般的清潔。
generatedExample: Many officials were arrested during the political purges.

explanationZhTw: 「concrete evidence」表示「確鑿的證據、具體而明確的證據」；這裡的 concrete 不是「混凝土」。
generatedExample: We need concrete evidence before we make an accusation.

explanationZhTw: 「fragment」是「碎片、片段」；這裡指一小部分殘存下來的過去。
generatedExample: Only a fragment of the letter survived.

explanationZhTw: 「abolished」通常是「廢除」；這裡是比喻性的說法，指「被政權抹去、消滅的過去」。
generatedExample: The museum displayed a map of the abolished city.

explanationZhTw: 「turns up」是片語動詞，意思是「出現、被找到、冒出來」。
generatedExample: My old passport turned up in a drawer yesterday.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「徹底摧毀、炸得粉碎」；這裡強調如果公開，會對政黨造成毀滅性打擊。
generatedExample: One leaked document could blow the whole scheme to atoms.

explanationZhTw: 「significance」是「重要性、重大意義」；這裡指這項證據的政治重量。
generatedExample: The significance of her statement became clear later.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「一直繼續工作，沒有停下來」；這裡的 `straight on` 強調動作持續不中斷。
generatedExample: She went straight on typing even after the meeting ended.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「把它蓋住、遮掩起來」，也可引申為「隱瞞」。這裡是先用另一張紙把照片遮住。
generatedExample: He quickly covered the note up with a magazine.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「顛倒的、上下相反的」。在這裡表示從電幕的角度看，內容是倒著的。
generatedExample: The map was upside-down, so we turned it around.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `pushed back` 是片語動詞，意思是「把椅子往後推」。不是抽象的「反對」或「推回去」，而是很具體的動作。
generatedExample: She pushed back her chair and stood up.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `pick it up` 不是「撿起來」，而是指「偵測到、聽到、察覺到」他的心跳。
generatedExample: The microphone picked it up even though he was whispering.

explanationZhTw: `go by` 在這裡表示時間「流逝、過去」。
generatedExample: Three hours went by before she noticed.

explanationZhTw: `all the while` 是慣用語，意思是「整個過程中一直、始終」。
generatedExample: He kept calm all the while, even though he was nervous.

explanationZhTw: `draught` 在英式英文裡指「穿堂風、冷風氣流」。這裡是指一陣風可能會讓他暴露。
generatedExample: Close the window, or the draught will make the room cold.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `betray` 不是一般的「背叛」，而是「洩露、暴露」他的秘密或行蹤。
generatedExample: One small mistake could betray their hiding place.

explanationZhTw: `memory hole` 是小說中的特定用語，指用來丟棄並銷毀文件的通道或裝置。
generatedExample: The report disappeared into the memory hole.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「保留、留下」的意思，指把照片留著而不是丟掉。
generatedExample: She kept the ticket as a reminder of the trip.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「奇怪的、耐人尋味的」意思，不是「好奇的」。
generatedExample: It was curious that nobody answered the phone.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「有影響、會改變結果」或「有差別」。
generatedExample: A few words of support can make a difference.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「記錄下來」的意思，指照片把那個事件保存成記憶。
generatedExample: The video recorded the whole conversation.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「對……的掌控／影響力」，不是單純的「拿著」。
generatedExample: The old story still has a strong hold upon many readers.

explanationZhTw: 「證據、佐證」；這裡指可支持某種判斷的材料。
generatedExample: The witness gave evidence that helped the case.

explanationZhTw: 「resurrected」在這裡不是字面上的「復活」，而是指「重新被找回、恢復到可用/可被認知的狀態」。
generatedExample: If the old map were resurrected from the archive, it might change how we understand the town's history.

explanationZhTw: 「evidence」指「證據、佐證」，用來支持某個主張或判斷。
generatedExample: The broken phone became important evidence in the investigation.

explanationZhTw: 「agents」在這裡是「特務、派來執行任務的人」，不是一般的「代理人」。
generatedExample: The journalist believed the men were agents of a foreign power.

explanationZhTw: 「betrayed」表示「背叛、出賣」，通常指違反信任或效忠。
generatedExample: He was accused of betraying his country by passing secrets to the enemy.

explanationZhTw: 「rewritten」在這裡表示內容被一再改寫，帶有刻意改動歷史或敘事的意味。
generatedExample: The report was rewritten and rewritten until it matched the official story.

explanationZhTw: 「significance」指「重要性、意義」，常用來說明某事是否有分量或影響。
generatedExample: The small delay had no real significance for the final result.

explanationZhTw: 「afflicted」表示「使深受困擾、折磨」，語氣比一般的 hurt 或 bother 更重。
generatedExample: The uncertainty afflicted her with constant anxiety.

explanationZhTw: 「imposture」是「偽裝、欺騙性的假象」，比一般的 lie 更正式，也更強調整體騙局。
generatedExample: The entire campaign was built on a huge imposture.

explanationZhTw: 「falsifying」表示「竄改、偽造」，在這裡特別指故意改動歷史或事實。
generatedExample: Someone had been falsifying the past by changing old records.

explanationZhTw: 「ultimate」在這裡表示「最終的、根本的」，用來指最深層或最後的原因。
generatedExample: We understood the immediate benefit, but the ultimate motive was still unclear.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 understand 是基本動詞「理解、明白」；前半句表示我懂方法，後半句表示我不懂原因。
generatedExample: I understand the instructions, but I still do not understand the reason.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 HOW 指「方式、方法」，重點是事情怎麼做，不是單純的疑問詞。
generatedExample: She explained how the machine works.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 understand 是基本動詞「理解、明白」；前半句表示我懂方法，後半句表示我不懂原因。
generatedExample: I understand the instructions, but I still do not understand the reason.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 WHY 指「原因、理由」，重點是為什麼會這樣，而不是怎麼做。
generatedExample: He asked why the meeting was canceled.

explanationZhTw: lunatic 指「瘋子、精神失常的人」；這裡是敘述者自我懷疑的說法，帶有強烈的負面評價。
generatedExample: He started to fear that people would think he was a lunatic.

explanationZhTw: minority of one 是慣用說法，意思是「只有自己一個人持這種看法」；強調觀點上的孤立感。
generatedExample: In that meeting, she felt like a minority of one.

explanationZhTw: unalterable 表示「不可改變的、無法修改的」；這裡指過去已經不能再被改動。
generatedExample: The date on the document is unalterable.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 alone 不是單純「獨自」做事，而是指「只有他一個人這麼相信」；是帶有孤立、異類感的用法。
generatedExample: She was alone in thinking the plan would fail.

explanationZhTw: trouble 這裡是動詞，意思是「使困擾、使煩惱」；greatly 用來加強程度，不是「很多麻煩」的字面意思。
generatedExample: The delay did not greatly trouble him.

explanationZhTw: 書籍封面的插圖頁或扉頁；此處指內頁前面的圖版。
generatedExample: The frontispiece showed a map of the lost kingdom.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「催眠」，而是指眼神極具吸引力、讓人無法移開注意。
generatedExample: Her hypnotic voice made the room go silent.

explanationZhTw: 指感官所提供的證據，也就是你親眼所見、親耳所聞所形成的判斷。
generatedExample: In the fog, the evidence of our senses was hard to trust.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻式說法，表示明顯荒謬、違反事實的結論卻被要求接受。
generatedExample: They tried to make the bad data look true, as if two and two made five.

explanationZhTw: 不可避免的；事情照理一定會發生。
generatedExample: Given the weather, a delay was inevitable.

explanationZhTw: validity 指「有效性、正當性、成立性」；這裡是指經驗作為認知依據是否可靠。
generatedExample: We questioned the validity of the survey results.

explanationZhTw: 指外在世界、獨立於心智而存在的現實。
generatedExample: Philosophers often debate whether external reality can be known directly.

explanationZhTw: tacitly 指「默默地、未明說地」；整句表示這種否認不是公開宣告，而是暗中成立。
generatedExample: The policy tacitly accepted that the old rules no longer worked.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「異端中的異端」；這裡是反諷，表示最不可容忍、最顛覆體制的觀念。
generatedExample: In that group, doubting the leader was the heresy of heresies.

explanationZhTw: 指常識、合乎理性的基本判斷；此處帶有反諷，表示連常識都被視為危險。
generatedExample: Common sense told her to leave before the storm got worse.

explanationZhTw: `courage` 指「勇氣、膽量」；這裡是指他原本動搖的勇氣又突然振作起來。
generatedExample: She needed courage to tell the truth.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，意思是「自己突然變得堅定起來」；`of its own accord` 表示「自然而然地、自己就發生」。
generatedExample: My resolve seemed to stiffen of its own accord when I heard the bad news.

explanationZhTw: `called up` 在這裡不是「打電話叫來」，而是「喚起、引出（記憶、畫面、想法）」。
generatedExample: The old song called up memories of my childhood.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用的形象說法，表示「突然浮現在腦海中」。
generatedExample: An idea floated into her mind during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `on his side` 表示「支持他、站在他這邊」。
generatedExample: I was relieved to know that my teacher was on my side.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是他把日記當成寫給某個特定對象的文字，而不是單純私人記錄；`TO O'Brien` 強調收信人／對象。
generatedExample: She wrote the note for her future self, to herself.

explanationZhTw: `interminable` 指「冗長得似乎沒完沒了的、長到令人厭煩的」。
generatedExample: We sat through an interminable meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單指信封上的地址，而是「寫給某個特定的人、以某人為對象」。
generatedExample: The speech was addressed to a particular person in the audience.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，意思是「因為那個事實而帶上了特定的性質、語氣或風格」。
generatedExample: His apology took its colour from his guilt and fear.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思不是一般「證據」的法律用法，而是指你親眼看到、親耳聽到的事實依據；整句是在要求你否定自己的感官判斷。
generatedExample: You should not reject the evidence of your eyes and ears when the result is obvious.

explanationZhTw: 表示突然感到沮喪、失望或不安，好像情緒一下子往下掉。
generatedExample: My heart sank when I saw the broken screen.

explanationZhTw: 指有一股明顯而強大的力量站在對立面，像是已經排好陣勢準備對抗他。
generatedExample: The whole team seemed arrayed against her in the final round.

explanationZhTw: 用來強調前面那件事都做不到，更不用說後面更難的事。
generatedExample: I can barely finish my homework, much less start a new project.

explanationZhTw: 指那些人人都知道、很顯而易見卻仍然成立的道理或老生常談。
generatedExample: The article was full of truisms that everyone already knew.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「牢記、堅持住」的意思，不是單純拿住某個東西。
generatedExample: Hold on to that idea if the work gets difficult.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「提出、闡述」一個重要觀點或原則。
generatedExample: She began setting forth her plan for the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 指被視為基本真理或不需證明的原則，在這裡帶有正式、抽象的語氣。
generatedExample: The professor explained the axiom before moving on to the proof.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「自由、可自由表達的狀態」。在這句話中，它帶有政治/思想自由的抽象含義，不只是一般日常的「自由」感覺。
generatedExample: She values freedom more than comfort.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 granted 不是「授予」某個具體東西，而是「被承認、被接受成真」的意思。整句是在說：如果這個前提成立，其他結論就會自然成立。
generatedExample: If that is granted, the rest of the argument makes sense.
